task_completion.description: Open the Electrical Engineering Calculations app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Electrical Engineering Calculations app is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the motor efficiency calculator
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows a specific tool or section for calculating motor efficiency within the app.

task_completion.description: Enter the specified input and output power values
task_completion.visual_evidence: The values 10 (with HP as the unit for output power) and 8.5 (with kW as the unit for input power) are entered into the respective input fields.
The app displays the calculated efficiency result, which should be approximately 87.73%.

task_completion.description: Save the calculated efficiency to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager shows a file named 'efficiency_report.txt' (located in /sdcard/) containing the text 'Efficiency: 87.73%' (or a closely matching calculated value).

integrity.description: The agent used the app's GUI to perform the calculation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Electrical Calculations app to get the result, rather than skipping straight to a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interactions
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent using a terminal emulator (like Termux) to run echo commands or python scripts to calculate or create the file.

integrity.description: The saved result matches the calculated result
integrity.visual_evidence: The efficiency percentage written in the text file exactly matches the value that was displayed on the app's calculator screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected efficiency is approximately 87.73% (acceptable range: 86.23% - 89.23% due to a 1.5% tolerance). The calculation uses 10 HP for output power and 8.5 kW for input power. The final output must be saved to /sdcard/efficiency_report.txt in the exact format: 'Efficiency: <value>%'.
